meta.description: End-to-end checklist for a public safety agency standing up a DFR program from zero to operational. Covers policy, certification, airspace, procurement, integration, training, community, and governance.

items.item: Define mission scope and use cases
items.detail: Identify which 911 call types will trigger DFR dispatch. Common: active shooter, pursuit, missing person, structure fire recon, traffic accident. Scope determines platform, dock placement, and waiver terms.
Assess current response times, call volume by type, areas where DFR would add most value. Use this data in grant narrative and FAA safety case.
Define geographic coverage area. Check for Class B/C/D/E airspace, LAANC availability, nearby airports, GA traffic patterns. Use FAA SkyVector or Airspace Link.

items.item: Select FAA authorization pathway (Part 107 waiver vs COA vs both)
items.detail: Most programs start with Part 107 BVLOS waiver — currently averaging under 1 week approval. COA for persistent/expanded operations.
Strongly recommended for first program. Flying Lion, SkyfireAI, or SkyFire Consulting have application track records. Reduces rejection risk significantly.

Submit via FAA DroneZone. Include safety case, platform specs, SOPs, emergency procedures, mitigation measures (parachute, anti-collision lighting, DAA). Ensure application is complete — incomplete submissions are most common failure mode.
COPS Technology, HSGP/SHSP, UASI, state programs (Ohio model). Contact State Administrative Agency (SAA) for HSGP/UASI. Contact COPS Office directly for COPS Tech.
Defines: authorized use cases, prohibited uses, data retention (video footage), public records obligations, who can authorize launches, escalation procedures. DRONERESPONDERS has policy templates.

items.item: Define data retention and privacy policy
items.detail: How long is footage retained? Who can access it? What are public records obligations? Different from body cam policy — needs separate treatment. Some states have specific drone surveillance laws.

Who approves program changes? Is there civilian oversight? Some cities (Denver, Scottsdale) have faced council scrutiny for launching programs without public notice. Proactive governance prevents reactive problems.

items.item: Review applicable state drone surveillance laws
items.detail: Several states have laws restricting law enforcement drone surveillance (warrants, retention limits, prohibited uses). Check current state law before finalizing use policy.
Covers: dispatch trigger criteria, launch authorization, flight rules, abort procedures, lost-link procedures, post-flight documentation, incident reporting. Required for FAA waiver application.

items.item: Define public records / FOIA response procedures for drone footage
items.detail: Drone footage is public record in most jurisdictions. Define retention period, redaction process, and FOIA response workflow before first flight.

items.item: Confirm NDAA compliance for selected platform
items.detail: Verify platform is not DJI, Autel, or other covered entity. For Blue UAS-listed platforms: reference DIU list. For non-Blue platforms: obtain manufacturer NDAA certification letter.

items.item: Confirm AEL eligibility if using DHS/FEMA grants
items.detail: Reference AEL item 03OE-07-SUAS. Confirm with SAA before purchase. AEL items may change — verify current list.

items.item: Issue RFP or use cooperative purchasing vehicle
items.detail: Many agencies use cooperative purchasing (GSA Schedule, NASPO ValuePoint, Sourcewell) to streamline procurement. Check if selected vendor is on a cooperative contract.

items.item: Procure drone platform, dock, and accessories
items.detail: Budget template: platform $20–50K per unit, dock $30–80K, software licenses $5–20K/yr, training/FAA consulting $20–50K. Total single-site: $75–200K.
Skydio DFR Command (if Skydio platform), DroneSense (platform-agnostic), Flock911 (if Flock Alpha). Ensure CAD integration is configured before go-live.

items.item: Ensure all remote pilots hold FAA Part 107 certificate
items.detail: Part 107 is baseline requirement. If pilots don't have it, allow 2–3 months for study and exam.
Skydio, BRINC, Flock, Parrot all offer platform training. Required before operational use. Document completion for liability and insurance.

Part 107 cert alone is not sufficient for BVLOS ops. Flying Lion and SkyfireAI offer DFR-specific BVLOS training. FLI Town (Flying Lion dedicated DFR training center) is the most comprehensive option.

items.item: Train dispatch on DFR trigger criteria and launch authorization
items.detail: Dispatchers need to know which call types trigger DFR launch, how to monitor drone status, and how to relay aerial intel to responding units.

items.item: Conduct tabletop and live exercises before go-live
items.detail: Run through scenarios: active shooter, pursuit, missing person, structure fire. Identify gaps in SOPs, CAD integration, and communication before first real deployment.

items.item: Select and prepare dock installation site(s)
items.detail: Rooftop is most common. Assess structural load, weather exposure, power supply, cellular coverage, and coverage radius. Flying Lion and manufacturer can advise on placement.

items.item: Install dock and validate power and connectivity
items.detail: Ensure stable power and strong cellular signal at dock location. 4-modem cellular redundancy (Flock Alpha) or equivalent. Test launch cycle before go-live.

Connect DFR software to CAD system. For Skydio: Axon Evidence + Fusus + CAD. For Flock Alpha: Flock911 + PremierOne. For mixed/other: DroneSense. Test auto-dispatch or dispatch-triggered launch workflow.
Drone footage must flow to evidence management system with chain of custody. Critical for law enforcement use. Axon Evidence integration (Skydio) is purpose-built for this.
Ensure command staff and RTCC can view live drone feed. Fusus, DroneSense, or vendor platform handles this. Test before go-live.

items.item: Develop public communications plan before launch
items.detail: Denver PD faced backlash for launching without community notice. Scottsdale PD launched with proactive communication and had smooth rollout. Proactive transparency prevents reactive controversy.
Post on city/agency website. Include use cases, prohibited uses, data retention, oversight structure.

items.item: Present program to city council / oversight body
items.detail: Proactive council briefing prevents the surprise and controversy that hit Denver. Brief before launch, not after.
Publish monthly or quarterly reports: call volume, response times, outcomes. Data builds public trust and supports grant renewal narratives.

items.item: Soft launch — limited call types and geography
items.detail: Start with lower-risk call types (traffic, patrol) before deploying on active shooter or pursuit. Build operational confidence before high-stakes deployments.
Key metrics: response time vs ground units (% drone arrives first — target 70–80%), calls handled, outcomes (arrests, subjects located, fires assessed). Required for grant reporting and renewal.
Review every DFR deployment for SOP adherence, footage quality, CAD integration performance, and outcome. Improve continuously.
When FAA Part 108 drops (expected mid-2026), evaluate whether it expands or changes your operational authorization. Update waivers/COA as needed.

quick_reference.typical_standup_timeline: 4–6 months from decision to first operational deployment
quick_reference.fastest_possible: 2–3 months with experienced FAA consultant, pre-approved vendor, and existing budget authority
most_common_delays: Incomplete FAA waiver application (adds weeks of back-and-forth)
Procurement lead times (especially Inspired Flight — capacity constrained)
IT CAD integration (underestimated by most agencies)
